evidence: Each frame carries one clear focus: the retrieval card (f1), the two stacks (f2), the amber 'your taste' probe against the periwinkle shelf (f3-f4), and the winning bar with the 0.87 readout (f5-f6), with earlier elements receding to supporting weight.
Elements accrete gradually across frames — stacks, then arrows, then score bars, then the readout — never exceeding four novel elements introduced together at equal weight.
The only text label, 'your taste', sits directly above the amber probe arrow it names; no boxed legend or color key appears.
On-screen text is limited to the 'your taste' label on the amber arrow and the '0.87' score readout, both annotating visible referents; no spoken-register prose sits on the background.
'your taste' and '0.87' are both rendered well above ~2.2% of frame height and are clearly readable.
The periwinkle shelf trails off deliberately with an ellipsis ('. . .') rather than being hard-cropped, and all focal elements — card, stacks, probe, readout — sit fully inside the frame.
Arrows, bars, the label, and the readout occupy distinct rows and columns with no overlapping or intersecting elements.

Colors stay consistent to their referents: observed/catalog candidates in periwinkle blue, the amber 'your taste' probe as attention, and the winning score bar/readout in lilac; no token semantics are inverted within the Night School cast.
Every element — the retrieval card, arrows, score bars, and readout — carries a propositional role; no decorative mascots or ornaments appear.

The frames deliver the intent: the retrieval card idles (f1), the two stacks return beside their arrows with the amber probe labeled 'your taste' (f2-f3), the candidate row extends into a long periwinkle shelf running off frame (f3-f4), score bars rank under the shelf with the top slid forward (f5), and the readout fills with the winning 0.87 (f5-f6).
The 'your taste' label sits directly on its probe, score bars sit beneath the arrows they score, and the 0.87 readout sits above the winning bar it reports.
Across the six frames the diagram builds in reasoning order — card, stacks, arrows, extended shelf, ranked bars, winning readout — as continuous accretion rather than a whole diagram cut in.
The composition keeps generous margins and a clear left-to-right, top-to-bottom reading order with ample negative space around the readout and shelf.
